channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Canadian Firm Plans Domestic Production of Generic Ozempic to Increase Accessibility
item.description: A newly-formed Canadian pharmaceutical company, Vimy Pharma, is preparing to enter the market with a domestically-produced generic version of Ozempic, a well-known drug for diabetes and weight loss. F...

item.title: Ex-CJI Highlights Lack of Strong Measures for Gender Parity in Judiciary
item.description: On International Women's Day, former Chief Justice of India NV Ramana highlighted concerns over the underrepresentation of women in the higher judiciary. Speaking at the first national conference of '...

item.title: 25 Women’s Rights Activists Detained in Islamabad Over Section 144 Violation During Women’s Day Gathering
item.description: In Islamabad, police detained at least 25 women activists associated with the Aurat March as they gathered at Super Market in Sector F-6 to mark International Women’s Day. The authorities cited violat...

item.title: Foundation for Ethical Society and Cultural Awareness Launches Nationwide Programme to Promote Integrity and Civic Values in Nigeria
item.description: The Foundation for Ethical Society and Cultural Awareness (FESCA), one non-profit organisation wey dey focus on better ethics and culture for Naija, don officially launch their National Initiative for...

item.title: Trump Administration Weighs Potential Special Forces Operation to Secure Iran's Highly Enriched Uranium Stockpile
item.description: According to a Bloomberg News report cited by multiple sources including Reuters and HuffPost, U.S. President Donald Trump is evaluating the deployment of special operations forces inside Iran to seiz...

item.title: National government should stop duplicating county functions
item.description: Kenya’s 2026/27 budget reveals a country still trapped in a cycle of heavy borrowing and excessive recurrent spending. The government plans to spend Sh4.7 trillion, with only Sh749.5 billion allocated...

item.title: KSE-100 suffers major drop amid oil price surge and geopolitical tensions
item.description: Pakistan’s KSE-100 index experienced a sharp decline on Monday, losing 13,050.63 points or 8.29 per cent by 10:50am, shortly after trading resumed. Earlier, trading was halted around 9:20am after the ...

item.title: Canadian Government Proposes House of Commons Debate on Iran Conflict
item.description: Liberal House leader and Transport Minister Steven MacKinnon announced that Prime Minister Mark Carney’s government has suggested holding a debate in the House of Commons regarding the ongoing hostili...

item.title: Collingwood dominates opening round against St Kilda with strong teamwork
item.description: Collingwood kicked off the AFL season with a composed and strategic victory over St Kilda, proving many critics wrong after a patchy pre-season. While St Kilda entered the game with high expectations,...

item.title: Understanding Medieval Justice at a Reflective Museum in the United States
item.description: Travel offers opportunities to explore not only new places but also new perspectives on history. In the United States, some museums take a reflective approach by focusing on challenging historical sub...

item.title: Toxic black rain and air pollution after US airstrikes in Iran: health and environmental risks
item.description: Following recent US-Israeli airstrikes on oil depots in Iran, reports have emerged of black rain falling over parts of the country. Local residents have experienced headaches, breathing difficulties, ...

item.title: Matt Dunstone Captures First Brier Title with Manitoba Victory Over Kevin Koe
item.description: Matt Dunstone and his Manitoba curling team claimed their first Canadian men’s curling championship by defeating four-time champion Kevin Koe of Alberta 6-3 in St. John’s, Newfoundland and Labrador. A...

item.title: Professor Pate Remains Focused on Health Sector Reforms, Not Bauchi Governorship
item.description: Even as rumour dey circulate say Professor Muhammad Ali Pate fit wan run for Bauchi State governorship in 2027, correct information show say the Coordinating Minister of Health no don plan to resign o...

item.title: EACOP Launches 'Keep a Girl in School' Programme to Provide Sanitary Pads and Menstrual Hygiene Support in Tanzanian Schools
item.description: The East African Crude Oil Pipeline (EACOP) project has rolled out the first phase of its 'Keep a Girl in School' initiative as part of its Social Economic Investment and Corporate Social Responsibili...

item.title: OpenAI hardware head resigns over ethical concerns on US Defense Department partnership
item.description: Caitlin Kalinowski, head of OpenAI’s hardware team, resigned following the company's new agreement with the United States Department of Defense. In a social media post, Kalinowski emphasized that her ...

item.title: Justin Bieber Shows Frustration Toward Paparazzi Outside Sushi Park
item.description: Justin Bieber experienced a tense moment with paparazzi after a late-night dinner with his wife, Hailey Bieber, at Sushi Park in Los Angeles. As the couple exited the restaurant and approached their S...

item.title: Tehran Residents Face Toxic Fallout and Shortages Following Oil Depot Strikes
item.description: Tehran is reeling after airstrikes targeted four oil depots and a petroleum logistics site, leaving six dead and 20 wounded. The city awoke to thick black smoke, soot-covered streets, and potentially ...

item.title: Parliament resumes Budget Session with debate on Motion to remove Speaker Om Birla
item.description: The second part of the Budget Session of the Indian Parliament resumed on Monday, 9th March 2026, amid expectations of a highly charged debate. The first item on the agenda is the Motion of Removal ag...

item.title: US Contemplates Special Forces Operation Amid Rising Tensions with Iran
item.description: Tensions in the Middle East continue to escalate as the conflict enters its tenth day. Iran has designated Mojtaba Khamenei, son of the current supreme leader Ali Khamenei, as his successor, signallin...

item.title: Tanzanians Support Women's Right to Decide on Marriage and Family Size
item.description: Recent survey by Afrobarometer in Tanzania shows most citizens support women making their own decisions about marriage and how many children to have. About 77% agree girls and women should decide when...
